source_scope_policy.policy: Use context-only paths as evidence, but do not select them as first-slice targets when active core alternatives exist.

records.label: Flask-like map/data application

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is that route handling, map/geodata filtering, imports, and file/database side effects converge around {target}.

target_architecture_shape: HTTP routes stay thin and only validate request parameters plus choose the use case.
Application services own map viewport queries, incident filtering, import jobs, and export flows.
Adapters isolate filesystem, SQLite/GeoJSON/MBTiles, external geocoding, and subprocess work.
Pure geodata transforms such as bbox parsing and point/line filtering get contracts and fast tests.

first_slice.name: map_viewport_query_slice
first_slice.goal: Make the map request path explicit before touching import/download jobs.
steps: Define a ViewportQuery input contract from request args.
Extract bbox parsing and geometry filtering as pure capabilities.
Wrap incident/branch/ATM data fetch behind a read adapter.
Add contract tests for empty bbox, malformed bbox, and normal viewport result shape.

defer: Do not change map data formats, MBTiles/GeoJSON layout, or import scripts until the read/query slice is stable.
Do not add more download/geocoding behavior before existing side effects are wrapped.
records.verification_extra: Use fixture map/incident data to verify bbox filtering and serialized response shape.

records.label: FastAPI LLM gateway/proxy

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is the request-forward-normalize path around {target}, where retry, rate-limit state, and upstream failures need one explicit contract.

target_architecture_shape: The public API layer validates OpenAI-compatible requests and attaches request identity.
A provider selection layer maps model/provider policy without performing network I/O.
A transport adapter owns upstream calls, timeout, retry, rate-limit, and response normalization boundaries.
Replay/checkpoint data records request capture, selected provider, upstream status, and normalized response.

first_slice.name: chat_completion_proxy_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one request path from API input to normalized upstream response.
steps: Define RequestEnvelope and ProviderDecision contracts.
Keep provider selection pure and test it without network calls.
Wrap upstream forwarding with timeout, failure packet, and replay-safe request id.
Verify rate-limit state and normalized response with focused contract tests.

defer: Do not add more providers until provider selection and transport failure contracts are stable.
Do not tune rate limiting before request identity and replay semantics are explicit.
records.verification_extra: Use fake upstream transport to verify timeout/error normalization without a real provider call.

records.label: agent/advisory runtime

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is hidden control flow around {target}, where memory, tool selection, fallback, and execution decisions should be separated into named steps.

target_architecture_shape: Conversation/advisory entrypoints delegate to an explicit control loop.
Mode selection, memory append, capability acquisition, tool execution, and fallback are separate capabilities.
Each loop step emits a compact packet that can be replayed or stopped by policy.
Process boundaries isolate tool execution and generated plugin rendering from the main loop.

first_slice.name: advisory_control_loop_slice
first_slice.goal: Expose one advisory loop as named packets instead of implicit control flow.
steps: Define packet contracts for mode selection, memory append, execution request, and stop/continue.
Split side effects away from decision functions.
Persist enough step artifacts to replay a failed advisory request.
Add a controlled failure test for fallback and retry/stop behavior.

defer: Do not add new roles or tools until one advisory loop is replayable.
Do not merge memory mutation with control decisions.
records.verification_extra: Use a stored advisory request to replay stop/continue and fallback decisions.

records.label: HTTP CLI client

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is the CLI-to-HTTP execution path around {target}, where argument parsing, request construction, transport, output formatting, and retry/error behavior need explicit boundaries.

target_architecture_shape: CLI parsing produces a typed request command without performing network I/O.
Request construction and authentication live in a core HTTP request model.
Transport adapters own network calls, timeout, streaming, and error normalization.
Output formatting is isolated from request execution and can be tested with captured responses.

first_slice.name: cli_http_request_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one CLI request path from parsed arguments to normalized response/output.
steps: Define a typed CLIRequest or RequestCommand contract.
Split argument interpretation from HTTP request construction.
Wrap transport with timeout/error/result contracts.
Verify formatting with fixture responses and no live network dependency.

defer: Do not add new protocol features until one CLI request path is contract-tested.
Do not tune output themes before request construction and transport boundaries are explicit.
records.verification_extra: Use captured HTTP responses and fake transport to test success, timeout, and malformed input.

records.label: HTTP client library

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is the request-session-adapter path around {target}, where redirect handling, auth, transport errors, and response normalization need stable contracts.

target_architecture_shape: Public request helpers stay thin and delegate to session/request models.
Session orchestration owns redirects, cookies, auth, hooks, and retry decisions.
Transport adapters isolate network I/O, TLS/proxy behavior, timeout, and pool errors.
Response normalization and exception mapping are contract-tested without live network calls.

first_slice.name: http_session_transport_slice
first_slice.goal: Make one request execution path explicit from prepared request to adapter response.
steps: Define PreparedRequestExecution input and TransportResult output contracts.
Separate redirect/auth orchestration from adapter network execution.
Wrap adapter errors into a normalized failure packet.
Add contract tests with fake adapter responses and redirect/auth edge cases.

defer: Do not alter public request APIs before the session/adapter slice is characterized.
Do not rely on live HTTP services for the first verification pass.
records.verification_extra: Use fake adapters to verify redirect, auth, timeout, and response-shape contracts.

records.label: workflow orchestration runtime

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is workflow state transition around {target}, where scheduling, retry, trigger, checkpoint, and worker handoff need explicit contracts.

target_architecture_shape: Workflow definitions are separated from runtime scheduling and execution state.
Task/flow execution produces typed state-transition records before persistence or worker handoff.
Scheduler, trigger, worker, and storage boundaries are isolated behind adapters.
Retry, resume, cancellation, and terminal failure semantics are contract-tested.

first_slice.name: workflow_state_transition_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one workflow/task transition from trigger to scheduled/executed state.
steps: Define WorkflowRunRequest and WorkflowStateTransition contracts.
Separate workflow definition parsing from runtime scheduling decisions.
Wrap worker/storage side effects as explicit transition artifacts.
Verify success, retry, cancellation, resume, and failed-dependency paths.

defer: Do not rewrite the scheduler and worker runtime in one slice.
Do not change public DAG/flow APIs before state-transition contracts are characterized.
records.verification_extra: Use fake scheduler/storage/worker fixtures to verify workflow state transitions.

records.label: automation/playbook execution engine

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is automation execution around {target}, where inventory, variables, templating, module dispatch, remote I/O, and result normalization need explicit contracts.

target_architecture_shape: Playbook/inventory parsing creates typed execution plans before remote side effects.
Variable resolution and templating are separated from module dispatch.
Connection, filesystem, and subprocess boundaries are isolated behind adapters.
Idempotency, changed/failed status, and retry behavior are explicit result contracts.

first_slice.name: automation_task_execution_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one task/module execution path from resolved inputs to normalized result.
steps: Define AutomationTaskRequest and ModuleExecutionResult contracts.
Separate variable/template resolution from module side effects.
Make remote connection and filesystem operations adapter-owned.
Verify ok, changed, failed, skipped, and unreachable cases.

defer: Do not mix parser, variable resolution, and remote execution changes in one slice.
Do not execute live remote actions in first verification.
records.verification_extra: Use fake inventory and connection fixtures to verify task result contracts.

records.label: backup/archive tool

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is archive mutation around {target}, where repository locks, manifests, chunk I/O, encryption, and resume semantics need explicit contracts.

target_architecture_shape: Archive commands produce typed plans before repository mutation.
Repository, lock, chunk store, and encryption boundaries are explicit adapters.
Manifest updates and cache/checkpoint state are separated from data transforms.
Interrupted backup/restore paths are replay-safe and contract-tested.

first_slice.name: archive_repository_mutation_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one archive or restore mutation boundary.
steps: Define ArchiveOperationRequest and RepositoryMutationResult contracts.
Separate archive planning from repository/chunk side effects.
Record checkpoint/resume metadata for interrupted operations.
Verify success, missing chunk, lock conflict, and interrupted resume cases.

defer: Do not alter repository format or encryption behavior before adapter contracts exist.
Do not combine backup and restore refactors in one slice.
records.verification_extra: Use fixture repositories and fake chunk stores for mutation and resume tests.

records.label: application packaging/freezer

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is packaging graph construction around {target}, where import analysis, hooks, bootloader assets, filesystem output, and subprocess build steps need explicit contracts.

target_architecture_shape: Spec/import analysis builds a typed bundle graph before filesystem output.
Hook execution and environment discovery are isolated from graph normalization.
Bootloader/subprocess/filesystem boundaries are explicit adapters.
Platform-specific outputs are verified with deterministic fixtures.

first_slice.name: bundle_graph_analysis_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one import/spec analysis path into a bundle graph.
steps: Define BundleAnalysisInput and BundleGraph contracts.
Separate import/hook discovery from filesystem bundle emission.
Wrap subprocess and platform discovery behind adapters.
Verify missing module, hook failure, platform asset, and deterministic graph cases.

defer: Do not change bootloader build behavior before bundle graph contracts are stable.
Do not rely on host-specific filesystem state in first verification.
records.verification_extra: Use tiny fixture packages and fake hook/platform adapters.

records.label: proxy/security inspection tool

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is intercepted network flow around {target}, where TLS/cert handling, protocol parsing, mutation hooks, and replay safety need explicit contracts.

target_architecture_shape: Network flows are normalized before hook execution or UI/reporting.
TLS/certificate, socket, and protocol parsing boundaries are explicit adapters.
Flow mutation and replay decisions are separated from transport I/O.
Security-sensitive failures are quarantined with clear error classes.

first_slice.name: proxy_flow_normalization_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one intercepted flow from transport event to normalized flow record.
steps: Define RawProxyEvent and NormalizedFlow contracts.
Separate TLS/cert and protocol parsing from user hook mutation.
Classify transport, certificate, and parser failures explicitly.
Verify HTTP, TLS error, malformed input, and replay-blocked cases.

defer: Do not change live transport behavior before normalized flow contracts are tested.
Do not mix UI and proxy transport changes in one slice.
records.verification_extra: Use recorded/fake proxy events; do not require live network traffic.

records.label: desktop GUI/IDE application

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is UI/plugin orchestration around {target}, where widget state, plugin lifecycle, editor/LSP events, and filesystem/session state need explicit contracts.

target_architecture_shape: UI widgets emit typed commands/events rather than owning business decisions.
Plugin lifecycle and editor/LSP adapters are separated from presentation state.
Filesystem/session/preferences state uses checkpointable models.
GUI behavior is tested through command/event contracts before full UI tests.

first_slice.name: gui_plugin_event_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one plugin or editor event path into a typed command/result.
steps: Define UiEventCommand and PluginActionResult contracts.
Separate widget event handling from plugin/editor side effects.
Wrap LSP/filesystem/session operations behind adapters.
Verify event, missing plugin, failed LSP, and persisted-state cases.

defer: Do not rewrite UI layout and plugin lifecycle together.
Do not require a live display server for first contract verification.
records.verification_extra: Use headless command/event tests with fake widget and LSP adapters.

records.label: ML app/UI framework

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is app/component execution around {target}, where UI schema, user callbacks, queueing, file artifacts, and API transport need explicit contracts.

target_architecture_shape: Component/app declarations produce typed UI schemas before callback execution.
User callback execution, queueing, file artifacts, and API transport are separate services.
Frontend/backend schema compatibility is verified through contract fixtures.
Long-running inference and upload/download side effects have failure packets.

first_slice.name: ml_component_execution_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one component or callback execution path from UI request to response artifact.
steps: Define ComponentInvocation and ComponentResult contracts.
Separate UI schema normalization from callback execution.
Wrap queue, file, and network artifacts behind adapters.
Verify validation error, callback failure, queued execution, and artifact output cases.

defer: Do not change frontend protocol and callback runtime in one slice.
Do not require live model inference in first verification.
records.verification_extra: Use fake callbacks and fixture component schemas for backend/frontend contract tests.

records.label: distributed/dataflow compute graph

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is compute graph construction around {target}, where lazy graph nodes, partition metadata, scheduler handoff, and replay semantics need explicit contracts.

target_architecture_shape: User-facing operations build typed compute graph nodes before execution.
Graph normalization, partition metadata, and scheduler submission are separate services.
Filesystem/network/distributed scheduler boundaries are adapters.
Deterministic graph shape and replay behavior are contract-tested.

first_slice.name: compute_graph_construction_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one lazy operation into a deterministic compute graph contract.
steps: Define ComputeGraphRequest and NormalizedGraph contracts.
Separate graph construction from scheduler execution.
Capture partition/chunk metadata as explicit schema fields.
Verify deterministic graph, invalid partition, scheduler failure, and replay cases.

defer: Do not alter distributed execution while graph construction contracts are unstable.
Do not mix array/dataframe and scheduler refactors in one slice.
records.verification_extra: Use small fixture arrays/dataframes and fake scheduler adapters.

records.label: web framework library

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is framework control flow around {target}, where app setup, routing/blueprint registration, request context, sessions, and CLI helpers need explicit contracts.

target_architecture_shape: Public framework objects delegate setup and dispatch decisions to small internal services.
Routing and blueprint registration have explicit data contracts independent from request execution.
Request/session context management is separated from user callback execution.
CLI/debug helpers read framework state but do not own core routing behavior.

first_slice.name: framework_routing_registration_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one framework setup path around app/blueprint registration and route visibility.
steps: Define a RouteRegistration or BlueprintRegistration contract.
Separate registration validation from mutation of app routing state.
Capture request/session side effects as explicit framework state changes.
Verify duplicate routes, missing endpoints, and session persistence with focused tests.

defer: Do not refactor request dispatch and CLI helpers in the same slice.
Do not change public extension hooks until registration contracts are locked.
records.verification_extra: Use minimal app fixtures to test blueprint registration, route listing, and session behavior.

records.label: CLI framework library

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is command declaration and invocation flow around {target}, where decorators, callback metadata, parameter parsing, context, and terminal I/O should be contract-separated.

target_architecture_shape: Command declaration metadata is separated from terminal I/O and invocation.
Argument/option parsing produces typed invocation data before callbacks run.
Context propagation and error rendering are explicit framework services.
Terminal editing/prompting is isolated behind adapters for deterministic tests.

first_slice.name: cli_invocation_contract_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one command declaration-to-invocation path.
steps: Define CommandDeclaration and InvocationContext contracts.
Separate declaration parsing from callback execution.
Wrap terminal I/O and editor/prompt behavior behind adapters.
Verify missing option, invalid type, and callback execution paths with fixture CLI runs.

defer: Do not redesign terminal UI and declaration parsing in one slice.
Do not use docs examples as core capability targets unless source package targets are available.
records.verification_extra: Use isolated CLI runner fixtures for parsing, callback, and error-rendering contracts.

records.label: terminal rendering library

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is render traversal/output flow around {target}, where object inspection, style state, terminal capabilities, and export formats need stable contracts.

target_architecture_shape: Renderable objects produce an intermediate render tree before terminal/export output.
Console state and terminal capabilities are passed as explicit context.
Pretty-print traversal and syntax/highlight logic are pure or fixture-driven where possible.
Export adapters own SVG/text/HTML serialization boundaries.

first_slice.name: terminal_render_pipeline_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one render path from object/input to render segments/export output.
steps: Define RenderInput, RenderContext, and RenderSegments contracts.
Separate traversal/measurement from terminal or export serialization.
Wrap terminal capability detection as an adapter.
Verify plain text, styled text, and export output using golden fixtures.

defer: Do not change every output format before one render path is characterized.
Do not mix terminal capability detection with object traversal refactoring.
records.verification_extra: Use golden render fixtures for plain, styled, and exported outputs.

records.label: logging library

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is logging dispatch around {target}, where sink configuration, record construction, formatting, exception rendering, and concurrency boundaries need contracts.

target_architecture_shape: Logger API calls build typed log records before sink dispatch.
Sink configuration and lifecycle are separated from message formatting.
Exception formatting is a pure transform over captured traceback data where possible.
Concurrency/async boundaries are isolated behind sink adapters.

first_slice.name: logging_record_dispatch_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one log call path from message input to sink dispatch.
steps: Define LogEvent and SinkDispatch contracts.
Separate record construction from sink lifecycle and formatting.
Wrap async/threaded sink behavior with explicit execution policy.
Verify message formatting, exception rendering, and sink failure cases.

defer: Do not change sink lifecycle and exception rendering in the same first slice.
Do not treat documentation examples as core logging flow.
records.verification_extra: Use fake sinks and captured exceptions to test formatting, dispatch, and sink failure.

records.label: embedded document database library

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is table/query flow around {target}, where query matching, document identity, storage persistence, and mutation semantics need explicit contracts.

target_architecture_shape: Table APIs expose query/search contracts before storage mutation details.
Query predicates operate as pure transforms over document snapshots.
Storage adapters own file/memory persistence and serialization errors behind a separate boundary.
Update/remove operations define idempotency and document identity semantics after the query contract is characterized.

first_slice.name: document_query_contract_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one document query path from condition/predicate to matched document result before changing storage mutation behavior.
steps: Define QueryRequest, QueryPredicate, and QueryResult contracts.
Separate query matching from storage read/write operations.
Treat storage adapters and table mutation as downstream side-effect evidence.
Verify match, no-match, malformed condition, and storage failure cases.

defer: Do not redesign all query operators before one query/search slice is contract-tested.
Do not change storage formats or mutation semantics before the query contract is explicit.
records.verification_extra: Use memory and temporary-file storage fixtures to test query matching, no-match behavior, mutation side effects, and persistence failures separately.

records.label: in-process scheduler library

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is job scheduling flow around {target}, where time calculation, job selection, execution, and error/retry behavior need explicit contracts.

target_architecture_shape: Job definitions are separate from clock/time calculation.
Due-job selection is deterministic for a supplied clock.
Job execution and error handling are isolated from schedule mutation.
Time zone and interval parsing are covered by contract tests.

first_slice.name: scheduler_due_job_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one scheduling path from job definition to next-run/due-job execution.
steps: Define JobSpec and ClockSnapshot contracts.
Separate next-run calculation from job execution.
Make due-job selection deterministic under a fake clock.
Verify interval, at-time, missed run, and failing job scenarios.

defer: Do not change public fluent API before the scheduling calculation contract is stable.
Do not mix timezone parsing with execution policy in the first slice.
records.verification_extra: Use fake clock fixtures for next-run, due-job, and missed-run tests.

records.label: environment configuration library

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is .env discovery/read/write flow around {target}, where parsing, filesystem search, environment mutation, and rewrite semantics need explicit contracts.

target_architecture_shape: .env parsing produces typed key/value records independent of process environment mutation.
File discovery and rewrite operations are filesystem adapters with explicit failure packets.
Environment variable mutation is a separate side-effecting boundary.
CLI commands delegate to parser/discovery/update services.

first_slice.name: dotenv_parse_rewrite_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one .env path from file discovery/parsing to key read or rewrite.
steps: Define DotenvDocument and EnvMutation contracts.
Separate parsing from filesystem discovery and process environment mutation.
Wrap rewrite operations with atomicity and failure semantics.
Verify malformed line, missing file, existing key, and rewrite cases.

defer: Do not change CLI behavior before parse/rewrite contracts are explicit.
Do not mutate process environment in tests that can use captured mutation plans.
records.verification_extra: Use temporary .env fixtures to test parse, lookup, rewrite, and missing-file behavior.

records.label: ORM/database library

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is query/session/transaction flow around {target}, where model metadata, connection ownership, transaction boundaries, and persistence errors need explicit contracts.

target_architecture_shape: Public query/model APIs build declarative operations before touching connection lifecycle.
Condition/filter construction remains testable without a live database where possible.
Session or unit-of-work logic owns identity map, transaction state, flush/commit/rollback decisions.
Engine/connection adapters isolate driver I/O, pooling, timeout, and dialect errors.

first_slice.name: query_session_transaction_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one query construction and session execution path before changing transaction mutation behavior.
steps: Define QueryOperation, QueryCondition, and TransactionResult contracts.
Separate query construction from session/connection side effects.
Treat flush/commit as downstream mutation boundaries with explicit rollback behavior.
Verify query, empty result, rollback, and connection failure cases.

defer: Do not change public model APIs before one query/session contract is characterized.
Do not mix migration metadata changes with runtime transaction refactoring.
records.verification_extra: Use fake or in-memory database fixtures for query, transaction, rollback, and driver-error paths.

records.label: async worker/queue system

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is worker job flow around {target}, where queue ownership, retry, acknowledgement, checkpoint, and poison-message handling need explicit contracts.

target_architecture_shape: Queue ingestion produces typed JobEnvelope records before execution.
Worker execution, retry policy, acknowledgement, and failure classification are separate services.
Broker/process boundaries are isolated behind adapters with timeout and reconnect behavior.
Checkpoint/replay state records job id, attempt, result artifact, and terminal failure reason.

first_slice.name: worker_job_execution_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one worker path from queued job envelope to ack/retry/failure result.
steps: Define JobEnvelope and JobExecutionResult contracts.
Separate broker I/O from job execution and retry decisions.
Record checkpoint/replay metadata for each attempt.
Verify success, retryable failure, poison message, and broker failure cases.

defer: Do not introduce new worker pools before acknowledgement and retry semantics are explicit.
Do not combine broker reconnection and task business logic in the first slice.
records.verification_extra: Use fake broker/queue fixtures for ack, retry, poison-message, and resume behavior.

records.label: web scraper/crawler

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is fetch-parse-schedule flow around {target}, where URL scheduling, fetch policy, parser drift, cache, rate limits, and output normalization need explicit boundaries.

target_architecture_shape: URL scheduling and frontier state are separated from network fetch execution.
Fetch adapters own HTTP policy, robots/rate limits, cache, timeout, and retry failures.
Parsers transform captured HTML/response fixtures into typed extracted items.
Output pipelines normalize, deduplicate, and persist items with replayable artifacts.

first_slice.name: fetch_parse_normalize_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one crawl path from URL request to parsed normalized item.
steps: Define CrawlRequest, FetchResult, and ParsedItem contracts.
Separate fetch policy from parser logic.
Use captured HTML fixtures for parser contract tests.
Verify timeout, parser drift, duplicate item, and cache-hit behavior.

defer: Do not tune crawling breadth before one fetch/parse path is fixture-tested.
Do not rely on live websites for default verification.
records.verification_extra: Use captured HTML and fake HTTP responses for parser, retry, rate-limit, and cache behavior.

records.label: dataframe/ETL pipeline

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is data lifecycle flow around {target}, where raw input, parsed frame, normalized data, cache/report artifacts, and schema drift need explicit contracts.

target_architecture_shape: Raw input acquisition is stored separately from parsed tabular data.
Parsing, normalization, validation, aggregation, and report writing are separate pipeline stages.
Schema and dtype expectations are explicit and testable.
Cache/checkpoint artifacts are keyed by input hash and transform version.

first_slice.name: raw_to_normalized_dataset_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one dataset path from raw input to normalized validated table.
steps: Define RawDataset, ParsedFrame, and NormalizedDataset contracts.
Separate parsing from normalization and report writing.
Record checkpoint/cache keys for raw and normalized artifacts.
Verify malformed input, schema drift, empty data, and normal aggregation cases.

defer: Do not optimize performance before schema and checkpoint contracts are explicit.
Do not mix report formatting with data normalization in the first slice.
records.verification_extra: Use small fixture datasets to test parse, schema validation, normalization, and checkpoint reuse.

records.label: test framework library

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is collection-fixture-execution flow around {target}, where plugin hooks, fixture resolution, test scheduling, assertion rewriting, and reporting need contracts.

target_architecture_shape: Test collection produces a typed TestItem graph before execution.
Fixture resolution is separate from test function invocation and plugin hook dispatch.
Assertion rewriting/reporting are explicit transforms over captured execution outcomes.
Plugin hooks are treated as extension boundaries with compatibility tests.

first_slice.name: test_collection_fixture_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one path from test discovery to fixture-bound test invocation.
steps: Define TestItem, FixtureRequest, and TestExecutionResult contracts.
Separate collection from fixture resolution and execution.
Wrap plugin hook calls with compatibility/error contracts.
Verify missing fixture, failing test, plugin hook, and reporting cases.

defer: Do not alter assertion rewriting and fixture resolution in one first slice.
Do not treat framework self-tests as first-slice targets when core source targets exist.
records.verification_extra: Use miniature test packages to verify collection, fixture resolution, plugin hooks, and failure reports.

records.label: packaging/build backend

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is metadata-to-artifact flow around {target}, where source tree discovery, metadata contracts, isolated builds, subprocess execution, and artifact validation need explicit boundaries.

target_architecture_shape: Project metadata parsing is separated from artifact generation.
Build isolation/subprocess execution is wrapped with timeout and captured logs.
Wheel/sdist artifact writers emit manifest and validation results.
Installer/resolver behavior is separate from build-backend protocol handling.

first_slice.name: package_metadata_artifact_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one build path from project metadata to validated artifact manifest.
steps: Define ProjectMetadata and BuildArtifactManifest contracts.
Separate metadata parsing from artifact writing and subprocess execution.
Capture build logs, artifact paths, and validation findings as replay artifacts.
Verify missing metadata, build failure, malformed artifact, and happy path cases.

defer: Do not add resolver behavior before metadata/artifact contracts are stable.
Do not execute unbounded build subprocesses without timeout and captured logs.
records.verification_extra: Use fixture pyproject trees to verify metadata parsing, artifact manifest, build failure, and validation behavior.

records.label: code formatter/static analysis tool

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is parse-analyze-transform-report flow around {target}, where source discovery, AST/token analysis, rewrite decisions, diagnostics, and file output need explicit contracts.

target_architecture_shape: Source discovery produces typed file inputs before parsing or analysis.
Parsing/tokenization is separated from formatting or diagnostic decisions.
Rewrite/report generation is isolated from filesystem mutation.
Cache and incremental state are keyed by file hash, options, and tool version.

first_slice.name: source_parse_analyze_report_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one source-file path from input discovery to diagnostics or formatted output.
steps: Define SourceFileInput and AnalysisOrRewriteResult contracts.
Separate parsing/tokenization from decision and output stages.
Wrap filesystem writes behind dry-run and atomic-write adapters.
Verify syntax error, unchanged file, changed file, and diagnostic output cases.

defer: Do not tune style or diagnostic rules before source parse/result contracts are stable.
Do not mutate files in default tests when dry-run output can be asserted.
records.verification_extra: Use fixture source files to test parse errors, unchanged output, rewritten output, and diagnostics.

records.label: ASGI/WSGI server runtime

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is protocol lifecycle flow around {target}, where socket/process ownership, request protocol adaptation, concurrency, timeout, and shutdown behavior need explicit contracts.

target_architecture_shape: Server startup owns config, sockets, workers, and lifecycle state behind a runtime boundary.
Protocol adapters convert ASGI/WSGI events to typed request/response envelopes.
Concurrency, timeout, backpressure, and graceful shutdown are policies, not hidden side effects.
Health, logs, and lifecycle events are replayable artifacts for failure analysis.

first_slice.name: request_protocol_lifecycle_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one protocol request path from accepted connection/event to response or shutdown packet.
steps: Define ProtocolEvent and ServerLifecycleResult contracts.
Separate socket/process startup from request protocol adaptation.
Represent timeout, cancellation, and shutdown as explicit failure packets.
Verify request, disconnect, timeout, and graceful shutdown cases with fake protocol events.

defer: Do not tune worker scaling before lifecycle and timeout contracts are stable.
Do not depend on real network sockets for first-slice verification.
records.verification_extra: Use fake ASGI/WSGI event streams to verify lifecycle, timeout, and response behavior.

records.label: template rendering engine

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is parse-context-render flow around {target}, where template loading, context validation, escaping, cache keys, and output formatting need stable contracts.

target_architecture_shape: Template loading returns a typed TemplateSource with origin and cache identity.
Parsing and compilation are separated from render-time context evaluation.
Escaping and trust policy are explicit and negative-tested.
Render output is deterministic for a given template version, context, and environment options.

first_slice.name: template_parse_render_slice
first_slice.goal: Make one template path explicit from source loading to rendered output.
steps: Define TemplateSource, RenderContext, and RenderResult contracts.
Separate loader/cache lookup from parsing and rendering.
Make escaping/trust policy visible in the contract.
Verify missing template, invalid context, escaping, and stable render output cases.

defer: Do not optimize template cache before source identity and context contracts are stable.
Do not change public syntax before parser/render behavior is characterized.
records.verification_extra: Use fixture templates to test missing variables, escaping, loader failure, and deterministic output.

records.label: auth/security library

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is identity-token-permission flow around {target}, where secret handling, session lifecycle, trust boundaries, and denial semantics need explicit contracts.

target_architecture_shape: Identity extraction is separated from credential verification and permission decisions.
Token/session lifecycle owns expiry, refresh, revocation, and replay/idempotency policy.
Secrets and cryptographic material never leak into logs, reports, or replay artifacts.
Negative authorization decisions are first-class results, not incidental exceptions.

first_slice.name: identity_token_validation_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one auth path from credential/token input to identity or denial result.
steps: Define CredentialInput, IdentityPrincipal, and AuthDecision contracts.
Separate parsing from verification and permission checks.
Add redaction policy for secrets in logs and artifacts.
Verify expired token, malformed token, denied permission, and happy path cases.

defer: Do not add providers before one token/session contract is stable.
Do not record raw secrets in checkpoint or replay artifacts.
records.verification_extra: Use fake keys/tokens to verify expiry, malformed input, denied permission, and redaction.

records.label: observability/metrics/tracing system

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is event-to-export flow around {target}, where event shape, metric cardinality, sampling, sink failures, and backpressure need explicit contracts.

target_architecture_shape: Instrumentation events have typed shape and stable semantic attributes.
Metric aggregation and trace/span construction are separated from exporter I/O.
Sampling, cardinality limits, and redaction are explicit policies.
Exporter failures degrade safely and never break core application execution.

first_slice.name: event_to_metric_export_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one telemetry event path from instrumentation input to export result.
steps: Define TelemetryEvent and ExportResult contracts.
Separate event normalization from aggregation and exporter I/O.
Represent exporter failure as a degraded packet with bounded retry.
Verify cardinality guard, redaction, exporter failure, and happy path cases.

defer: Do not add new sinks before one event/export contract is stable.
Do not allow telemetry export failure to fail the primary workflow.
records.verification_extra: Use fake exporters to verify dropped events, retry bounds, redaction, and successful export.

records.label: scientific/numerical compute library

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is array-input-compute-result flow around {target}, where shape/dtype contracts, numerical stability, backend policy, and reproducibility need explicit verification.

target_architecture_shape: Inputs declare shape, dtype, units, missing-value policy, and backend expectations.
Numerical kernels are separated from file/network adapters and plotting/reporting.
Randomness, tolerance, and backend selection are explicit and reproducible.
Large intermediate arrays are checkpointed by metadata/hash rather than copied blindly.

first_slice.name: array_input_compute_result_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one numerical path from array-like input to computed result.
steps: Define ArrayInput and ComputeResult contracts with shape/dtype/tolerance.
Separate numerical kernel from data loading and output formatting.
Record random seed, backend, and tolerance in verification artifacts.
Verify bad shape, NaN/missing data, tolerance boundary, and happy path cases.

defer: Do not optimize backend dispatch before shape/dtype contracts are stable.
Do not compare floating outputs without explicit tolerance.
records.verification_extra: Use small deterministic arrays to verify shape errors, tolerance, NaN policy, and reproducibility.

records.label: protocol/API client library

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is request-transport-response flow around {target}, where request construction, auth, retries, rate limits, response normalization, and error taxonomy need contracts.

target_architecture_shape: Request builders create typed envelopes before transport execution.
Auth, retry, rate-limit, and timeout policies are separate from response parsing.
Transport adapters return normalized success/failure results without leaking provider-specific exceptions.
Replay artifacts capture request identity and sanitized response/error metadata.

first_slice.name: request_transport_response_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one API call path from request builder to normalized response or failure.
steps: Define ApiRequestEnvelope and ApiResponseResult contracts.
Separate auth/retry/rate-limit policy from transport execution.
Normalize provider errors into a stable error taxonomy.
Verify timeout, rate limit, malformed response, and happy path with fake transport.

defer: Do not add more provider endpoints before one request/response contract is stable.
Do not call live APIs in first-slice verification.
records.verification_extra: Use fake transports to verify timeout, retry, rate-limit, malformed response, and sanitized artifacts.

records.label: schema/validation library

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is schema-to-validation flow around {target}, where input coercion, error collection, defaults, and serialized output need explicit contracts.

target_architecture_shape: Schema/model declarations stay separate from runtime validation and serialization.
Validation returns structured findings with paths, codes, and original input evidence.
Coercion/default policies are explicit and versioned.
Contract tests cover invalid type, missing field, unknown field, and nested schema errors.

first_slice.name: schema_validation_contract_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one validation path from raw payload to valid object or structured errors.
steps: Define RawPayload, ValidatedObject, and ValidationFinding contracts.
Separate schema discovery/declaration from runtime validation.
Make coercion, defaults, and unknown-field policy visible in the result.
Verify nested errors, missing fields, bad types, and stable serialization.

defer: Do not redesign public schema syntax before one validation result contract is characterized.
Do not collapse validation errors into free-form strings.
records.verification_extra: Use fixture payloads to verify error paths, coercion/defaults, and serialization stability.

records.label: cloud/API SDK

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is service-client request flow around {target}, where generated models, auth/session state, retries, endpoints, and response normalization need contracts.

target_architecture_shape: Service/resource model loading is separated from request execution.
Session, credential, endpoint, retry, and rate-limit policies are explicit.
Transport adapters normalize provider errors without leaking raw service shapes.
Generated service metadata is treated as versioned artifacts with compatibility tests.

first_slice.name: sdk_service_call_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one SDK operation path from operation model to normalized result.
steps: Define ServiceOperationRequest and ServiceOperationResult contracts.
Separate model/endpoint resolution from transport execution.
Represent credential, throttling, and service errors as stable failure packets.
Verify malformed input, fake service error, throttling, and successful response.

defer: Do not add service-specific behavior before the generic operation contract is stable.
Do not call live cloud APIs in first-slice verification.
records.verification_extra: Use fake service models and fake transport to verify auth/error/retry paths.

records.label: async protocol runtime

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is async protocol flow around {target}, where cancellation, backpressure, handshake state, and timeout semantics must be explicit.

target_architecture_shape: Protocol events are modeled as typed packets independent from socket I/O.
Cancellation, timeout, backpressure, and close semantics are policies with test fixtures.
Connection/session state is explicit and replayable.
Transport errors are normalized before reaching higher-level protocol logic.

first_slice.name: async_protocol_event_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one async protocol path from event/handshake to result or controlled close.
steps: Define ProtocolEvent, ConnectionState, and ProtocolDecision contracts.
Separate socket transport from protocol state transitions.
Represent cancellation, timeout, and close as structured outcomes.
Verify handshake failure, cancellation, timeout, and happy message flow.

defer: Do not tune performance before protocol state and cancellation contracts are stable.
Do not rely on real sockets for the first verification slice.
records.verification_extra: Use fake async event streams to verify handshake, timeout, cancellation, and close behavior.

records.label: documentation/static site generator

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is config-markdown-render-output flow around {target}, where config parsing, navigation, plugin hooks, and filesystem writes need contracts.

target_architecture_shape: Site configuration parsing is separated from page discovery and rendering.
Markdown/page transforms produce typed page artifacts before filesystem output.
Theme/plugin hooks are explicit extension boundaries with failure packets.
Output manifests record generated files, hashes, and skipped assets.

first_slice.name: site_page_render_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one documentation page path from config/source page to output artifact.
steps: Define SiteConfig, SourcePage, and RenderedPageArtifact contracts.
Separate config/nav parsing from markdown transformation and output writing.
Wrap plugin/theme hooks as explicit adapters with failure results.
Verify missing config, malformed markdown, plugin failure, and generated output manifest.

defer: Do not rewrite the plugin system before one page-render contract is stable.
Do not mutate output directories without an artifact manifest.
records.verification_extra: Use fixture docs trees to verify config errors, page rendering, plugin failure, and output manifest.

records.label: visualization/plotting library

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is figure-to-render flow around {target}, where artist state, backend policy, layout, and output serialization need explicit contracts.

target_architecture_shape: Plot/figure construction produces explicit scene or artist state before backend rendering.
Backend selection, font/layout policy, and output format are separate adapters.
Rendering side effects write through artifact manifests rather than hidden filesystem state.
Golden fixtures cover layout stability, unsupported backend, and output serialization.

first_slice.name: figure_render_artifact_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one plotting path from figure state to rendered artifact manifest.
steps: Define FigureState, RenderOptions, and RenderArtifact contracts.
Separate artist/figure state mutation from backend rendering.
Wrap backend/font/output failures as structured findings.
Verify minimal plot, invalid backend, layout drift, and output manifest cases.

defer: Do not change every backend before one render artifact contract is stable.
Do not use large binary outputs as primary human-readable evidence.
records.verification_extra: Use small deterministic plots and artifact manifests for backend and layout verification.

records.label: media processing library

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is media decode-transform-encode flow around {target}, where file/codec boundaries, subprocess calls, large artifacts, and deterministic output need contracts.

target_architecture_shape: Media loading/decoding is separated from pure transforms and encoding/output.
Codec, duration, size, format, and color/audio policies are explicit inputs.
Subprocess or native backend execution is isolated with timeout and captured logs.
Large artifacts are tracked by manifest/hash rather than embedded in reports.

first_slice.name: media_decode_transform_encode_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one media path from input artifact to transformed output manifest.
steps: Define MediaInput, TransformSpec, and MediaOutputManifest contracts.
Separate decode/load, transform, and encode/write stages.
Wrap codec/backend failures and subprocess timeouts as failure packets.
Verify bad file, unsupported codec, small fixture transform, and output manifest.

defer: Do not optimize codec/backend performance before output contracts are stable.
Do not load large binary artifacts into human reports.
records.verification_extra: Use tiny fixture media/images to verify decode errors, transforms, encoding, and manifest hashes.

records.label: signing/token utility

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is sign-serialize-verify flow around {target}, where secret material, expiry, tamper detection, algorithm policy, and error taxonomy need explicit contracts.

target_architecture_shape: Payload serialization is separated from cryptographic signing and verification.
Secret/key material, algorithm choice, salt/audience, expiry, and clock policy are explicit inputs.
Tamper, expiry, malformed token, and wrong-key outcomes are stable denial/failure results.
Logs, reports, and replay artifacts never contain raw secrets or trusted payloads unless redacted.

first_slice.name: sign_verify_contract_slice
first_slice.goal: Stabilize one signing path from payload to token and back to verified payload or denial.
steps: Define SignedPayloadInput, TokenEnvelope, and VerificationResult contracts.
Separate payload serialization from signing/verifying and secret lookup.
Represent tamper, expiry, malformed token, and wrong-key cases as structured outcomes.
Verify happy path, tampered token, expired token, malformed token, and redaction behavior.

defer: Do not add new algorithms or token formats before one sign/verify contract is stable.
Do not persist raw secrets or unredacted signed payloads in reports or replay artifacts.
records.verification_extra: Use fake keys and deterministic clocks to verify signing, expiry, tamper detection, malformed input, and redaction.

records.label: code generation/plugin factory

diagnosis.template: {label} with entrypoint {entrypoints}; the main risk is renderer/codegen drift around {target}, so generated artifacts need a shared input/output contract before more generators are added.

target_architecture_shape: A generator contract defines RenderInput, RenderedArtifact, validation findings, and deterministic metadata.
Renderer modules become focused template/capability implementations behind that contract.
Build/package orchestration remains outside renderer functions and records artifacts for replay.
Golden-output and negative contract tests guard generator drift.

first_slice.name: generator_contract_slice
first_slice.goal: Make one generator path contract-driven before expanding renderer coverage.
steps: Introduce a minimal RenderInput -> RenderedArtifact contract for one generator.
Move template rendering details behind the contract.
Record generated file list and validation findings as replay artifacts.
Add golden-output tests and one malformed input test.

defer: Do not refactor all renderers at once; prove the generator contract on one representative renderer first.
Do not change public package shape until generated artifact compatibility is checked.
records.verification_extra: Use golden generated output plus schema validation for generated artifact metadata.
